laws.title: Public Law 23-171 | Diplomatic Immunity Act
laws.status_basis: Source is usable but text_extracted is false; the law text is effectively image-only/scanned and no operative provisions were recovered. The enactment cannot be classified beyond source-limited historical status.

provisions.text_summary: Text not recovered from the scanned source.
provisions.evidence: Manifest shows valid PDF, text_extracted=false, image_only_or_scanned=true.

current_implementation.detail: A note anchor exists in current XML, but the recovered source text is insufficient to verify any substantive codification result.

recommended_actions: keep only a source-limited historical note
do not assert any substantive Code effect
remove Trello link

laws.title: Public Law 23-172 | Congressional Military Service Act
laws.status_basis: Full text recovered. The act contains direct amendments to PL 18-140 sec.304(a) and Title 10 sections 12203, 12204, and 151, plus uncodified protections and effective-date language. Nothing in the source indicates repeal.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.evidence: SEC1 states the act may be cited as the Congressional Military Service Act (CMSA).

provisions.text_summary: Severability and immediate effective-date rule.
provisions.evidence: SEC2 makes the act severable; SEC3 makes it effective immediately upon enactment.

provisions.text_summary: Amends PL 18-140 sec.304(a) to allow limited enlistment and commissioning for certain public officials.
provisions.exact_change: Replace section 304(a) with the enacted restrictions and permissions for enlistment and commissioning up to O-6.
provisions.evidence: SEC4(1) says section 304(a) of PL 18-140, as amended by PL 23-166, shall be amended to state the new text.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals Title 10 section 12204.
provisions.evidence: SEC5(a) repeals section 12204 of Title 10 in its entirety.

provisions.text_summary: Rewrites Title 10 section 12203.
provisions.exact_change: Replace section 12203 with the enacted subsections governing service members and related restrictions.
provisions.evidence: SEC5(b) amends section 12203 of Title 10 with new subsections (a)-(c).

provisions.text_summary: Rewrites Title 10 section 151 governing the Joint Chiefs.
provisions.exact_change: Replace section 151 composition/function text with the enacted version, including Guard Bureau and Coast Guard language.
provisions.evidence: SEC9(a) amends section 151 of Title 10 with new Joint Chiefs composition and duties text.

provisions.text_summary: Protections, savings, and other uncodified limitations.
provisions.evidence: The source adds anti-retaliation and related protections that are not framed as positive-law section text.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML has a note anchor, but the express textual amendments to Title 10 and PL 18-140 sec.304(a) were not applied as section text.

recommended_actions: apply the direct amendments to Title 10 sections 12203, 12204, and 151
apply the PL 18-140 sec.304(a) replacement at its code home
keep the uncodified protections as concise statutory notes
remove Trello link

laws.title: Public Law 24-173 | Federal Enhancement Against Foreign Intrusion Act
laws.status_basis: Full text recovered. The act is a generally applicable foreign-relations/national-security enactment with no express numbered Code amendment, but it is not repealed and remains operative as statutory history.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, effective date, and definitions.
provisions.evidence: Section I contains the act's short title, severability clause, effective-date clause, and definitions.

provisions.text_summary: Bars federal employees from holding foreign-government posts.
provisions.evidence: Section II prohibits federal employees from holding positions inside foreign governments and disallows reliance on PL 22-163 sec.5.

provisions.text_summary: Bars elected officials from foreign-government or foreign-military employment.
provisions.evidence: Section III prohibits elected officials from subsequently holding employment in foreign governments or foreign military groups.

provisions.text_summary: Extends the same foreign-position bar to Senate-confirmed officials.
provisions.evidence: Section IV prohibits Senate-confirmed officials from holding foreign-government or foreign-military positions.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML preserves the act as a statutory note at a national-security anchor; no express Code section text needed because the source does not identify a determinable positive-law target.

recommended_actions: retain as a concise statutory note
do not fabricate a permanent Code section
remove Trello link

laws.title: Public Law 24-174 | Washington, D.C. Permitless Carry Act
laws.status_basis: Full text recovered. This is a local District of Columbia public-safety enactment with no express positive-law U.S. Code amendment, so it belongs as statutory history rather than permanent section text.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, purpose, and definitions.
provisions.evidence: The act identifies itself as the Washington, D.C. Permitless Carry Act and defines concealed firearm, eligible individual, member of Congress, federal judge, and federal law enforcement officer.

provisions.text_summary: Eligibility and location restrictions for permitless carry in D.C.
provisions.evidence: The act sets eligibility criteria and bans carry in specified government buildings and other locations.

provisions.text_summary: Special provisions for Congress, judges, law enforcement, parks, and enforcement.
provisions.evidence: The act grants special carry rules for members of Congress, federal judges, and federal law enforcement officers, and includes enforcement rules.

provisions.text_summary: Restoration of rights, supremacy clause, and Secret Service identification rules.
provisions.evidence: The source includes a rights-restoration provision, a supremacy clause, and Secret Service/ID restrictions.
provisions.notes: Source numbering has duplicate Section 9 labels.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML preserves the act as a DC statutory note at a defensible Title 40 anchor; the remaining defect is the Trello link and the unverified repeal/superseded label.

recommended_actions: keep as a concise DC statutory note
do not promote to permanent positive-law text
remove Trello link
verify any repealed/superseded labeling against external history

laws.title: Public Law 24-178 | Equality in Employment Act
laws.status_basis: Full text recovered. The act makes a direct, determinable amendment to 42 U.S.C. 2000e-2(a) by adding gender identity and sexual orientation as protected classes and expanding the discrimination text.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, effective date, and findings/sense provisions.
provisions.evidence: These sections introduce the act and provide non-codified findings and implementation direction.

provisions.text_summary: Amends 42 U.S.C. 2000e-2(a) to add gender identity and sexual orientation.
provisions.exact_change: Add gender identity and sexual orientation to the protected characteristics in subsection (a)(1) and (a)(2).
provisions.evidence: SEC6(a) expressly directs amendment of section 703(a) of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, 42 U.S.C. 2000e-2(a).
provisions.notes: Current XML already reflects the text change in Title 42.

provisions.text_summary: EEOC and Labor implementation directives.
provisions.evidence: The act directs EEOC enforcement support and Labor rulemaking.

provisions.text_summary: Conclusion/sense of Congress.
provisions.evidence: The final section is a concluding sense statement rather than positive-law text.

current_implementation.detail: The substantive Title 42 amendment is present in the XML, but the codification note is mis-anchored to Title 29 and carries a full-text dump that should be removed.

recommended_actions: keep the Title 42 amendment as applied text
remove the full-text dump and Trello link from the note
move or replace the note at the correct Title 42 anchor
retain the EEOC/Labor directives as concise statutory notes

laws.title: Public Law 25-179 | Fiscal Year 21-2
laws.status_basis: Full text recovered. The act is an appropriations bill for a short fiscal period and is time-limited by its own terms, so it should not become permanent Code text.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and immediate effective-date rule.
provisions.evidence: Sections 1 and 2 supply the title and immediate-effect language.

provisions.text_summary: Appropriations and reporting provisions.
provisions.evidence: The act appropriates funds for the stated short fiscal period and contains agency spending/reporting directions.
provisions.notes: Do not promote appropriations to permanent section text.

provisions.text_summary: Temporary COLA suspension under 2 U.S.C. 4501.
provisions.exact_change: For FY 21-2, no adjustment under 2 U.S.C. 4501.
provisions.evidence: SEC5 expressly suspends COLA adjustments for the fiscal year.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note is an acceptable appropriations/history note, but the act itself is temporary and should not be treated as permanent Code text.

recommended_actions: keep only a concise historical note
exclude appropriations from permanent Code text
record SEC5 as a temporary note at 2 U.S.C. 4501
remove Trello link

laws.title: Public Law 25-180 | Sanction Repealment Act
laws.status_basis: Full text recovered. The act repeals PL 9-55 and declares sanctions on Iran null and void. It is a repealing act, not a self-repealed one.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and purpose.
provisions.evidence: The act identifies itself as the Sanction Repealment Act and states its purpose to lift sanctions.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals PL 9-55 in its entirety.
provisions.evidence: SEC3(b) expressly repeals Public Law 9-55 in its entirety.

provisions.text_summary: Declares sanctions on Iran null and void.
provisions.evidence: SEC3(c) declares all imposed sanctions on the Islamic Republic of Iran null and void.

provisions.text_summary: Severability and effective date.
provisions.evidence: The act includes severability language and immediate effectiveness.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML note is a reasonable foreign-relations anchor, but the repeal of PL 9-55 should also be recorded as a repeal-marking at the original Code home.

recommended_actions: record the PL 9-55 repeal as a repeal-marking at its Code home
keep the sanctions-nullification as a concise statutory note
remove Trello link
